• Per the Apr 28 2026 rep note from Guillaume Pasquet: 'Good clarification meeting on Sourcing for indirect full AI... metrics.narrative: WIK articulated qualitative pain but has not quantified savings, ROI, cycle-time improvement, or cost reduction. Next_Step__c 6 Mar records 'Interest in AI quick wins around negotiations,' positioning Merlin ANA as a fast-value use case. 27 Mar: 'ANA sounds a great use case for them,' confirming appetite. 28 Apr: 'Part of broader global digitalisation and strategic initiative as this Agentic AI use case will be the 1st one (Intake, ANA, Strategic Sourcing),' signaling strategic intent and first-mover AI positioning within WIK's transformation roadmap. Pain owner is Rouven Ehniss (GM Global Sourcing), who is driving the use-case validation and mgmt approval. However, no quantified business case (savings target, headcount efficiency, negotiation cycle compression) has been documented across Next_Step__c, tasks, or contact-role notes. Without a quantified value case, the EB approval and budget prioritization are at risk. The move is to co-build a quantified ROI model (cost avoidance per negotiation, cycle-time reduction, or supplier-response rate improvement) with Rouven and present it to mgmt alongside the pilot proposal.

sources: Next_Step__c 6 Mar to 2 Jul 2026: Rouven Ehniss
champion.narrative: Rouven Ehniss (General Manager - the deal owner's manager) is the developing champion. He is the primary Zycus contact (appears in 9 of 12 dated Next_Step__c entries from 6 Mar to 2 Jul), drove NDA execution (13-18 Mar), use-case validation (27 Mar to 14 May), and is now escalating the pilot proposal to mgmt for budget approval (6 Jun). Next_Step__c 14 May: 'Good engagement after presales meeting (appetite with Intake/ANA),' confirming active advocacy and positive sentiment. He holds the Evaluator contact role, signaling formal decision influence. However, three gaps prevent 'strong' champion status: (1) access to Economic Buyer is unconfirmed (he is escalating to 'mgmt' but has not introduced Zycus to the CFO/CEO/CPO); (2) multi-threading is absent (Legal, IT, Finance, and end-user stakeholders are contact roles but not engaged by Rouven on Zycus's behalf); (3) engagement is cooling: Rouven and Hagen declined Horizon 2 Jul ('they have declined Horizon, and will revert on the ANA project shortly'), and 1 day has elapsed with no reversion yet (early, but a disengagement signal). Next_Step__c 2 Jul shows rep-initiated follow-up ('follow up with Ehniss & Hagen for next steps'), not buyer-initiated contact, indicating Zycus is chasing, not being pulled. Champion strength is developing because Rouven has driven the process to this point, but his cooling engagement, lack of EB access, and single-threaded execution put the deal at risk. The move is to re-engage Rouven this week, understand the Horizon decline rationale and internal priority shift, confirm the mgmt approval timeline, and secure introductions to the CPO and Karen Zheng (Purchasing Controlling) to multi-thread before mid-July.

competition.narrative: No competitor is documented in Competitors__c, and no competitor name appears in any Next_Step__c entry, task, or contact-role note across 117 days. Next_Step__c 28 Apr describes the deal as 'Part of broader global digitalisation and strategic initiative as this Agentic AI use case will be the 1st one (Intake, ANA, Strategic Sourcing),' suggesting WIK is positioning Zycus as the flagship AI deployment, which could imply sole-sourced or first-mover advantage. However, the absence of competitive intel across all sources is itself a risk: either (1) WIK is truly sole-sourcing Zycus and no competitive pressure exists (positive, but unconfirmed), or (2) Zycus has not discovered the competitive landscape (blind spot). The Next_Step__c 27 Apr entry ('Decision to move forward based on requirements matching') implies a go/no-go gate rather than a multi-vendor bake-off, but without explicit confirmation from Rouven, the competitive posture is unknown. The move is to ask Rouven directly in the re-engagement call this week: 'Are you evaluating other vendors alongside Zycus, or is this a sole-source selection?' and 'What alternatives (build in-house, incumbent extension, do-nothing) are being considered by mgmt?' If a competitor exists, surface it and build a wedge; if sole-sourced, confirm it and lean into first-mover AI positioning.

identify_pain.narrative: WIK's pain is operational inefficiency and lack of AI-driven automation in procurement, specifically around supplier negotiations and intake/requisition processing. Next_Step__c 6 Mar: 'Interest in AI quick wins around negotiations,' articulated by Rouven Ehniss (GM Global Sourcing), identifying negotiation cycle length and manual supplier engagement as the pain point. 27 Mar: 'ANA sounds a great use case for them,' confirming Merlin ANA (Autonomous Negotiation Agent) as the highest-value pain-relief use case. 28 Apr: 'Part of broader global digitalisation and strategic initiative as this Agentic AI use case will be the 1st one (Intake, ANA, Strategic Sourcing). Decision in Q2,' positioning the pain within WIK's enterprise transformation roadmap and signaling strategic urgency (first AI deployment as a flagship initiative). 14 May: 'Good engagement after presales meeting (appetite with Intake/ANA). Scheduling meeting with delivery next week to manage paid POV,' confirming pain validation and appetite for a paid proof-of-value on Intake and ANA. The pain owner is Rouven Ehniss, who is driving the use-case selection and mgmt approval. However, the pain has not been quantified (no cost-of-delay, cycle-time baseline, or headcount-hours articulated), and no end-user pain validation (from Hazel Su or Aurora Shen, the sourcing practitioners) has been documented. The move is to quantify the negotiation-cycle pain (average cycle time, cost per negotiation, volume per month) and validate it with the sourcing team (Hazel, Aurora) to strengthen the ROI case for mgmt approval.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unfolding as: (1) NDA execution and use-case submission (completed by 18 Mar); (2) Zycus presales feasibility review and process mapping (completed by 28 Apr); (3) internal WIK mgmt budget approval (in progress since 6 Jun, pending as of 2 Jul); (4) pilot proposal and build strategy (proposed for 22 Jun Horizon discussion, but Horizon was declined 2 Jul). The approval chain is escalated to 'mgmt' above Rouven Ehniss (GM Global Sourcing), but the specific decision-makers (CFO, CEO, CPO) and approval thresholds are not documented. Next_Step__c 22 Jun mentions 'CPO coming to Horizon' and 'ANA proposal with Mgmt,' suggesting a two-tier approval (operational sponsor Rouven + executive/CPO sign-off), but the CPO declined and has not been engaged directly. No multi-approval or procurement-committee structure has been confirmed, though Karen Zheng (Purchasing Controlling), Josslyn Deng (Legal), and Kelvin (IT, per 6 Mar Next_Step__c) are contact roles and likely gate-keepers for budget, contract, and technical validation, respectively. None of these stakeholders appear in recent Next_Step__c or task history, signaling single-threaded execution risk. The process is informal (no formal RFP/committee structure documented), driven by Rouven, and gated on mgmt budget approval. The move is to map the approval chain explicitly with Rouven (who signs, what thresholds, any committee review) and engage the CPO and Karen Zheng directly to de-risk the mgmt approval gate before mid-July.

decision_criteria.narrative: WIK submitted two AI use cases for Zycus feasibility review (Next_Step__c 13 Mar: 'AI use cases to be received next week'; 18 Mar: 'AI use cases to be shared by end of month (NDA signed)'). Presales completed use-case mapping and alignment by 28 Apr ('Presales will map processes with solution (including Strategic Sourcing) and share next week'). 27 Apr: 'Decision to move forward based on requirements matching,' confirming that WIK's criteria centered on AI feasibility, process fit (Intake, ANA, Strategic Sourcing), and Zycus solution coverage. 14 May: 'Good engagement after presales meeting (appetite with Intake/ANA),' indicating positive validation on ANA and Intake use cases. However, two unresolved decision criteria remain: (1) regional AI compliance for Hong Kong and China (raised 6 Mar, still pending 117 days later), and (2) agentic sourcing delivery readiness for 2026 pilot kickoff (flagged 11 May: 'Delay in Agentic Sourcing may impact final decision'). These are blocking criteria; if either is not satisfied, the decision will slip or the scope will narrow (dropping Strategic Sourcing from the pilot). No formal RFP, RFI, or scored evaluation matrix has been documented, but the use-case validation process and presales alignment served as the functional decision-criteria gate. The move is to close both open criteria: provide written confirmation on regional AI compliance (including any architecture mitigations if restrictions exist) and a committed agentic sourcing delivery timeline with product roadmap milestones, both by mid-July.
